A Practical Guide to Building High-Performance Web Applications with Less JavaScript, Faster Rendering, and Better User Experience

6 min read
Just now

--

Not A Premium Member? No Worries- Click Here To Read For Free.
For years, modern web development followed a familiar pattern.
Applications built with frameworks like React, Vue, or Angular typically rendered an entire page as one large JavaScript application.
This approach made building interactive user interfaces easier, but it also introduced a new problem.
As applications grew larger, so did the amount of JavaScript sent to the browser.
The result was often:

• slower page loads

• delayed interactivity

• poor mobile performance

• lower search engine rankings

• higher infrastructure costs
Developers responded by introducing techniques such as:

• code splitting

• lazy loading

• server-side rendering (SSR)

• static site generation (SSG)
These improvements certainly helped.
However, another architectural pattern has gained significant attention in recent years because it addresses…
